definition: Abrustdonis is described as a sacred cosmic substance that exists throughout the Universe. It is generally found blended with two other sacred substances, 'askokin' and 'helkdonis'. This substance, along with 'helkdonis', is explicitly stated to enter into the formation and perfecting of the higher being-bodies of three-brained beings, specifically the 'kesdjan body' and the 'body of the soul'. The separation of 'askokin' from 'abrustdonis' and 'helkdonis' occurs when beings transmute these sacred substances within themselves for the purpose of forming and perfecting their higher bodies, through conscious labor and intentional suffering.
role: A sacred cosmic substance essential for the formation and perfecting of higher being-bodies (kesdjan body and body of the soul).

key_quotes.text: His Conformity also explained that this cosmic substance, the sacred 'askokin,' exists throughout the Universe, generally blended with the sacred substances 'abrustdonis' and 'helkdonis,' and therefore, in order to have the degree of vivifyingness required for such maintenance, the sacred substance 'askokin' must first be freed from the other two substances.

I learned that these sacred substances 'abrustdonis' and 'helkdonis' are precisely those substances which enter into the formation and perfecting of the higher being-bodies of the three-brained beings—that is, the 'kesdjan body' and the 'body of the soul'—and that the separation of the sacred 'askokin' from the two other substances proceeds when beings, on whatever planet they may be, transmute these sacred substances in themselves for the forming and perfecting of their higher bodies, by means of conscious labor and intentional suffering.

definition: Absoizomosa is a state, defined by Objective Science, that a 'surplanetary formation' is reduced to when it has been artificially grafted or manipulated. In this state, the formation absorbs cosmic substances from its surrounding medium, but these substances are useful only for the coating of what is called its 'automatically reproducing subjective presence', indicating a limited or altered functional capacity compared to its natural state.
role: A state of reduced functional capacity in artificially manipulated surplanetary formations, where substance absorption is limited to maintaining its subjective presence.

key_quotes.text: when any surplanetary formation is artificially grafted or manipulated in any such fashion, it is reduced to a state defined by Objective Science as 'absoizomosa,' in which it absorbs from its surrounding medium cosmic substances useful only for the coating of what is called its 'automatically reproducing subjective presence.'

definition: Actavas are a type of 'being-apparatuses' on the planet Karatas, representing one of two different sexes (the other being 'passavas') through whom the completing process of the sacred Heptaparaparshinokh for the continuation of the species is carried out. They are described as transforming one kind of 'being-exioëhary' as the 'affirming principle'. On the planet Earth, 'actavas' are equivalent to 'man' or beings of the 'male sex'.
role: A type of being, specifically the male sex on Karatas, that serves as the 'affirming principle' in the process of species continuation.

key_quotes.text: the completing process of the sacred Heptaparaparshinokh for the continuation of the species is carried out not through one being, as with the tetartocosmoses, but through two beings of different sexes, called by us 'actavas' and 'passavas,' and on the planet Earth, 'man' and 'woman.'
one kind is transformed as affirming principle through 'beings-apparatuses'' known on our planet Karatas as 'actavas' and on your planet Earth as beings of the 'male sex'

definition: Agoorokhrostini is the name for one of three special, very imposing buildings that were required to be built in every region and even in separate districts on the continent of Atlantis. These buildings were considered sacred by the Atlantian beings, functioning as their equivalents of 'temples,' 'churches,' or 'chapels'. Specifically, the 'agoorokhrostinis' were designated for 'beings of the male sex' of a given region or district, where they performed appropriate 'mysteries' while in a particular state called 'self-remembering'.
role: A sacred temple or building on Atlantis designated for men, where they performed mysteries in a state of self-remembering.

key_quotes.text: One, for the beings of the male sex, was called 'agoorokhrostini .'

The first two of these imposing buildings were considered sacred by the beings of the continent of Atlantis, representing for them what 'temples,' 'churches,' 'chapels,' and other sacred places are for the contemporary beings of the Earth.

In the temples for men, namely in the 'agoorokhrostinis,' beings of the male sex of the given region or district performed in turn appropriate 'mysteries' while in a particular state called 'self-remembering.'

definition: Ahoon is a character in the narrative, explicitly described as Beelzebub's devoted old servant who always accompanies him. He is a three-brained being who sits with Beelzebub and Hassein on the ship Karnak, engages in conversation, and follows Beelzebub's suggestions. Beelzebub refers to him as 'our old Ahoon,' and Hassein expresses love for him, believing that Ahoon helped him become worthy of witnessing Beelzebub's glorification. Ahoon exhibits a distinct personality, capable of speaking, showing particular postures, and experiencing inner psyche and feelings.
role: Beelzebub's devoted old servant and companion.

key_quotes.text: As this narrative begins, Beelzebub, Hassein, and Ahoon, the devoted old servant who always accompanied Beelzebub everywhere, were sitting on the highest "kasnik," or upper deck, of the Karnak under the "kalnokranonis," a sort of glass bell, talking among themselves while observing the boundless space.

First of all, I wish to voice in the language of our venerable Mullah Nasr Eddin the thought that arises in me by association about Ahoon's words, so unusual for him, and also about his unaccustomed posture.
What, my boy! Can it be that the same thing is proceeding in your presence as in our old Ahoon's?

This impulse of love is growing stronger in me because, it seems to me, both Ahoon and the three-brained beings of the planet Earth have greatly helped me to become worthy of being an eyewitness of the glorification of him who is the cause of the cause of my arising and who until now I have called 'my dear grandfather,' but who has now become,

definition: Aimnophnian is an adjective used to describe a specific type of 'being-mentation' or 'perceptive logic' observed in the three-brained beings referred to as 'your favorites' (Earth beings). This 'aimnophnian being-mentation' is characterized as deteriorated, superpeculiar, and exceptionally distorted, particularly in how these beings picture their God. This distortion is said to have originated from 'hasnamussian manifestations' and 'maleficent fictions' invented by 'learned' beings in Babylon, which were widely spread and absorbed.
role: A descriptor for a distorted and deteriorated form of perceptive logic or mentality in beings.

key_quotes.text: their 'aimnophnian being-mentation,' or 'perceptive logic,' has deteriorated to such a point that in their picturings they can see this God of theirs very clearly, with a little comb sticking out of His left vest pocket, with which He sometimes combs His famous beard

This superpeculiar aimnophnian being-mentation about their God proceeded in your favorites chiefly as a result of the hasnamussian manifestations of those 'learned' beings who, as you remember, assembled in the city of Babylon and together began inventing various maleficent 'fictions' about that God of theirs, which by chance were afterward spread everywhere on that ill-fated planet, and since at that period the three-brained beings there were existing in a particularly 'selzelnualnian' manner, that is, particularly 'passively' as regards the being-efforts proper to three-centered beings, they thoroughly absorbed and appropriated these maleficent fictions.

Later, in their transmission from generation to generation, these fictions were gradually crystallized into such monstrous 'logicnestarian material' that, in the psyche of the contemporary three-brained beings, there began to proceed this exceptionally distorted, aimnophnian being-mentation.

definition: Aisorian refers to a specific type of priest. One particular Aisorian priest is mentioned as residing on the continent of Asia in the locality called 'Urmia.' This priest was known to be in possession of half of an original 'boolmarshano' created by Makary Kronbernkzion, which he considered a large 'shapeless mass of ivory,' very ancient and valuable.
role: A descriptor for a type of priest from Urmia, Asia.

key_quotes.text: Having learned in this way that half of the copy of the original boolmarshano created by Makary Kronbernkzion was in the possession of an Aisorian priest, and that this priest existed on the continent of Asia in the locality called 'Urmia,' I went there and, having found him, soon ascertained that he did indeed possess what he called a large 'shapeless mass of ivory' that he considered very ancient and valuable.

definition: Akhldann primarily signifies a word that expressed the concept 'the striving to become aware of the sense and aim of the Being of beings.' It is also the name given to a 'learned Society of Akhldanns,' which was founded by Belcultassi on the continent of Atlantis 735 years before the second transapalnian perturbation. This society was composed of three-brained beings of the Earth who were convinced they were not as they ought to be and sought to understand the purpose of existence. Its members were initially called 'Akhldannsovors' and later divided into independent groups with specialized studies. Some members of the Society of Akhldanns survived the loss of Atlantis and helped preserve the 'sense and 'instinctive conviction' of the need for what is called 'completed personal being' for subsequent generations.
role: A concept meaning 'the striving to become aware of the sense and aim of the Being of beings,' and also the name of a society of three-brained beings on Earth dedicated to this striving.

key_quotes.text: And still later they founded the society which they named the 'Society of Akhldanns.' 'The word 'akhldann' then expressed the following concept: 'the striving to become aware of the sense and aim of the Being of beings.'

From the day of the founding of this society, Belcultassi himself stood at its head, and the activities of its members were carried out under his general guidance. For many of their years the society existed under the same name, and its members were called 'Akhldannsovors'; but later, when for purposes of a general character they divided up into a number of independent groups, the members came to be called by the names of their different groups.
this learned Society of Akhldanns, which arose on the continent of Atlantis and was composed of three-brained beings of the Earth, was founded 735 years before the second transapalnian perturbation.

The hope of the few surviving members of the Society of Akhldanns that they would be able to revive the society and carry out its tasks was not fulfilled Nevertheless, thanks to them alone, there was preserved in the presence of the beings of several generations after the loss of Atlantis the sense and 'instinctive conviction' of the need for what is called 'completed personal being.'

definition: Akhldann-fokhsovors were members of the first section of the Society of Akhldanns. Their specific intellectual occupation or study involved examining 'the presence of their own planet and the reciprocal action of its separate parts.'
role: Members of the Akhldann Society who studied their planet's presence and its interacting parts.

key_quotes.text: The members of the first section were called 'Akhldann-fokhsovors,' which meant that they studied the presence of their own planet and the reciprocal action of its separate parts.

definition: Akhldann-gezpoodjnisovors were members of the seventh and final group within the Akhldann Society. These members dedicated themselves to studying manifestations in three-brained beings of their planet that did not arise from various functionings coming from impulses generated by data already present in them. The passage provides an incomplete description of the full scope of their study.
role: Members of the Akhldann Society who studied specific manifestations of three-brained beings not caused by existing internal data.

key_quotes.text: And as regards the members of the seventh and last group, they were called 'Akhldann-gezpoodjnisovors.' These members of the Akhldann Society devoted themselves to the study of those manifestations of the three-brained beings of their planet proceeding in them not as a result of various functionings coming from impulses of different kinds engendered by data already present in them, but as a

definition: Akhldann-harnosovors were members of the fifth group within the Akhldann Society. Their particular intellectual pursuit or study focused on a branch of knowledge that combined the two contemporary terrestrial sciences known as 'chemistry' and 'physics.'
role: Members of the Akhldann Society who studied a combined form of chemistry and physics.

key_quotes.text: The members of the fifth group were called 'Akhldann-harnosovors,' which meant that they were occupied with the study of the branch of knowledge that combined the two contemporary terrestrial sciences called by your favorites 'chemistry' and 'physics.'

definition: The 'Akhldann-metrosovors' were members belonging to the third section of the Akhldann Society. They were beings occupied with the study of a branch of knowledge similar to 'silkoornano,' which corresponds in part to what is referred to as 'mathematics' by contemporary terrestrial beings.
role: Members of the Akhldann Society specializing in a branch of knowledge similar to mathematics.

key_quotes.text: The members belonging to the third section were called 'Akhldann-metrosovors,' which meant beings occupied with the study of that branch of knowledge similar to our 'silkoornano,' corresponding in part to what your contemporary favorites call 'mathematics.'

definition: The 'Akhldann-mistessovors' were members belonging to the sixth section of the Akhldann Society. They were beings who studied all kinds of outer events, whether these events were actualized consciously or arose by themselves. Additionally, they studied which of these events were erroneously perceived by beings and under what circumstances.
role: Members of the Akhldann Society who studied outer events and how they were erroneously perceived.

key_quotes.text: The members belonging to the sixth section were called 'Akhldann-mistessovors,' that is to say, beings who studied all kinds of outer events, whether actualized consciously or arising by themselves, and further studied which of these events were erroneously perceived by beings, and in what circumstances.

definition: The 'Akhldann-psychosovors' were members of the fourth group within the Akhldann Society. Their designation meant that they made observations of the perceptions, experiencings, and manifestations of beings like themselves, and they verified these observations statistically.
role: Members of the Akhldann Society who observed and statistically verified the perceptions, experiences, and manifestations of beings.

key_quotes.text: The members of the fourth group were called 'Akhldann-psychosovors,' a name designating those members of the society who made observations of the perceptions, experiencings, and manifestations of beings like themselves—observations that they verified statistically.

definition: The 'Akhldann-strassovors' were members of the second section of the Akhldann Society. Their role involved studying the 'radiations' of all other planets in their solar system and the reciprocal action of these radiations.
role: Members of the Akhldann Society who studied planetary radiations and their reciprocal action.

key_quotes.text: The members of the second section were called 'Akhldann-strassovors,' and this meant that they studied the 'radiations' of all the other planets of their solar system and the reciprocal action of these radiations.

definition: The 'Akhldanns' were the members of the 'Society of Akhldanns,' a learned society founded on the continent of Atlantis 735 years before the second transapalnian perturbation. This society was composed of three-brained beings of the Earth and was founded on the initiative of a being named Belcultassi. The word 'akhldann' itself expressed the concept of 'the striving to become aware of the sense and aim of the Being of beings.' After the loss of Atlantis, the few surviving members of the Society of Akhldanns were instrumental in preserving the 'sense and instinctive conviction' of the need for 'completed personal being' in subsequent generations.
role: Members of a learned society founded on Atlantis by three-brained beings, dedicated to understanding the sense and aim of Being and engaged in various scientific studies.

key_quotes.text: they founded the society which they named the 'Society of Akhldanns.'
The word 'akhldann' then expressed the following concept: 'the striving to become aware of the sense and aim of the Being of beings.'
this learned Society of Akhldanns, which arose on the continent of Atlantis and was composed of three-brained beings of the Earth, was founded 735 years before the second transapalnian perturbation.
It was founded on the initiative of a being there named Belcultassi

The hope of the few surviving members of the Society of Akhldanns that they would be able to revive the society and carry out its tasks was not fulfilled Nevertheless, thanks to them alone, there was preserved in the presence of the beings of several generations after the loss of Atlantis the sense and 'instinctive conviction' of the need for what is called 'completed personal being.

definition: The 'Akhldannsovors' were the original name for the members of the Society of Akhldanns. This name was used for many years after the society's founding. Later, when the society divided into a number of independent groups for purposes of a general character, the members began to be called by the names of their different specialized groups.
role: The initial, general name for the members of the Society of Akhldanns before their division into specialized groups.

key_quotes.text: For many of their years the society existed under the same name, and its members were called 'Akhldannsovors'; but later, when for purposes of a general character they divided up into a number of independent groups, the members came to be called by the names of their different groups.

definition: Aksharpantziar was a very well-known and aged Chaldean sage, described as a 'learned being' in his time. His report was the origin of all subsequent events relating to a 'famous contemporary art.' He proposed a new, artificial means of transmitting useful information and knowledge about past events to future generations through 'initiates of art,' 'works of the hands,' and various ceremonies, based on the Law of Sevenfoldness. His suggestion was unanimously accepted by the 'club of the Adherents of Legomonism.'
role: An aged, learned Chaldean sage whose report on an artificial method for transmitting knowledge to future generations formed the origin of subsequent events related to contemporary art.

key_quotes.text: learned being, very well known in those times, named Aksharpantziar.

As the report of that already very aged Chaldean sage, the great Aksharpantziar, was the origin of all the subsequent events relating to that famous contemporary art, I will try to recall his speech and repeat it to you as nearly as possible word for word.
With these words the great Aksharpantziar concluded his report.
then and there they unanimously decided to do as the great Aksharpantziar had suggested.

definition: 'All-Embracing Endlessness' is an appellation for the supreme Creator and maintainer of the Universe, also referred to as 'Endless Uni-Being,' 'All-Gracious Endless Creator,' 'Our Endlessness,' 'Omnipotent Creator,' 'All-Maintaining Endlessness,' 'Almighty Endlessness,' and 'Common Father Uni-Being Endlessness.' This entity actualized the 'common-cosmic trogoautoegocratic process.' Originally, He dwelt on the 'Most Great and Most Holy Sun Absolute.' When it was discovered that the Sun Absolute was diminishing in volume due to 'Heropass' (the flow of Time), the All-Embracing Endlessness was compelled to create the 'Megalocosmos' and all its contained cosmoses and formations. This creation was to establish a new system, called 'Trogoautoegocrat,' making the functioning of fundamental sacred laws dependent on external forces, thus maintaining the existence of the Sun Absolute.
role: The ultimate Creator and maintainer of the Universe, responsible for the common-cosmic trogoautoegocratic process and the creation of the Megalocosmos to sustain the Sun Absolute.

key_quotes.text: This most great common-cosmic trogoautoegocratic process was actualized by our Endless Uni-Being when there already existed the Most Great and Most Holy Sun Absolute, on which our All-Gracious Endless Creator had and still has the chief place of His existence.
our Endlessness was forced to create the whole world which exists at the present time

our Almighty Endlessness was then compelled to create our existing Megalocosmos with all the cosmoses of different scales and the relatively independent cosmic formations contained in it, and since then the system for maintaining the existence of the Sun Absolute has been called 'Trogoautoegocrat.'

Our Common Father Uni-Being Endlessness, having decided to change the principle of the maintenance of existence of that still unique cosmic concentration and sole place of His Most Glorious Being, first altered the functioning itself.

definition: Alla Ek Linokha is identified as a herdsman who possesses "inexhaustible wealth." His substantial wealth was a key factor that enabled large-scale investigations to be conducted.
role: A herdsman whose inexhaustible wealth facilitated large-scale investigations.

key_quotes.text: these investigations on a large scale thanks to the inexhaustible wealth of the herdsman Alia Ek Linokha, and the wonderful apparatus of the wise El Koona Nassa.

definition: Alla-Attapan is a "remarkable experimental apparatus" that became famous among scientists in the Megalocosmos. Beelzebub studied its construction and action, noting it consisted of three independent parts: 'loosochepana' (front), 'dzendvokh' (middle), and 'riank-pokhotartz' (rear). The 'loosochepana' processes daylight into a concentrated white ray, then splits it into seven colored rays using a crystal (which terrestrial beings call a 'prism'). The principles of the 'dzendvokh' part's construction were later used by the learned being King-Too-Toz to develop a theory of "evolution and involution of vibrations" and to create a related experimental apparatus called 'lav-mertz-nokh'.
role: A famous, three-part experimental apparatus studied by Beelzebub, used to split light and serving as a basis for scientific theories.

key_quotes.text: As my personal knowledge of the details of the construction and action of this remarkable experimental apparatus, alla-attapan, was due to accidental circumstances connected with my essence-friend Gornahoor Harharkh, and as it will surely be very interesting for you and at the same time exceedingly instructive, I shall describe it to you in some detail.
This famous experimental apparatus, alla-attapan, consisted of three independent parts.

Among these followers, about a century and a half after the sacred rascooarno of the saintly brothers, was a certain genuine learned being by the name of King-Too-Toz who, on the basis of the principles of construction of the middle part of the apparatus alla-attapan, named 'dzendvokh,' propounded a very detailed theory called the 'theory of the evolution and involution of vibrations', and to confirm this theory of his he made a special experimental apparatus which he named 'lav-mertz-nokh' and which later, by the way, also became known to almost all the learned beings of our Great Megalocosmos.

definition: Almacornian is an adjective used to describe a type of "turquoise." Its radiance is considered "famous and incomparable," and the entire space of the Great Universe, or the "whole sky," is said to reflect a radiance recalling that of almacornian turquoise when seen from a holy planet.
role: An adjective describing a type of turquoise, noted for its famous and incomparable radiance.

key_quotes.text: When we were there, you probably noticed that we always saw and sensed that the entire space of our Great Universe or, as your favorites would say, the 'whole sky,' seemed to reflect a radiance recalling that of the famous and incomparable 'almacornian turquoise ' Its atmosphere is always as pure as the 'phenomenal sakrooalnian crystal. '

definition: Almtznoshinoo is a "sacred process" or "sacrament" by which certain three-centered beings, who have perfected their own kesdjan bodies and Reason, can intentionally "materialize" or "coat" the kesdjan body of a being whose planetary body has ceased to exist. This process allows the materialized kesdjan body to manifest certain functions of its former planetary body for a limited time. It requires the higher being-body to have reached "completed functioning" and its Reason to have attained the degree of "being-mirotzinoo." Performers must consciously feed the kesdjan body with their "sacred 'aiëssakhladonn'." A crucial "preparation" involves removing a part of the being's 'being-hanbledzoin' (kesdjan body blood) during their planetary existence and preserving it. After death, this preserved part forms a temporary "threadlike connection" with the kesdjan body, lasting one planetary revolution around its sun. The "death and resurrection of Jesus Christ" is described as an event connected to the mystery and significance of this sacrament.
role: A sacred process for the temporary materialization of a being's kesdjan body after planetary death, associated with specific preparations and a cosmic event involving Jesus Christ.

key_quotes.text: I have this moment come to the conclusion that it will be very, very useful for your Reason if I tell you about another event connected with the mystery of the sacred 'almtznoshinoo,' which concerns that Sacred Individual whose conception was actualized among your favorites and who, after his formation, was named Jesus Christ.

The sacred 'almtznoshinoo' is the process by means of which certain three-centered beings, who have been able to attain the coating of their own kesdjan bodies up to completed functioning and to a definite degree of Reason, intentionally bring about the coating or, as is otherwise said, the 'materialization' of the 'kesdjan body' of a being whose planetary body has ceased to exist, up to such a density that this second body regains for a limited time the possibility of manifesting through certain functions proper to its former planetary body.

In order to understand the essence of this preparation for the sacred process of the 'sacrament of almtznoshinoo,' you need to know about two special properties of the 'being-hanbledzoin,' that is, the 'blood' of the kesdjan body.

In view of all this, the preparation required for the sacrament of the sacred almtznoshinoo consists in removing beforehand, during his planetary existence, a part of the hanbledzoin of the being on whose kesdjan body this sacred process is to be performed after his death, and this part must either be kept in some corresponding surplanetary formation, or be introduced into those beings who perform this 'ritual,' and intentionally blended with the hanbledzoin of their own kesdjan bodies.

definition: The Alnepoossian earthquake was a "planetary tremor" mentioned by the beings of Egypt. This earthquake caused the island then called 'Siapoora,' situated north of 'Cyprus,' to gradually sink into the planet over a period of five terrestrial years. During this process, it produced "extraordinary 'low' and 'high tides'" in the surrounding 'saliakooriap,' leading to vast quantities of sand rising to the surface and mixing with wind-blown sand.
role: A planetary tremor that caused the island of Siapoora to sink and produced unusual tides and sand accumulation.

key_quotes.text: I have already mentioned, and partly of a planetary tremor which was later called by the beings of Egypt the 'alnepoossian earthquake.'

During this planetary tremor, an island then called 'Siapoora,' situated to the north of a still existing island named 'Cyprus,' gradually, over a period of five terrestrial years, sank into the planet in a very peculiar way And while this process lasted, extraordinary 'low' and 'high tides,' as they are called, were produced in the great spaces of the surrounding 'saliakooriap,' as a result of which vast quantities of sand from beneath the saliakooriap rose to the surface of this terra firma, and became mixed with the sand blown there by the winds.

definition: The term "Alnokoorian" is not mentioned in the provided passages. Therefore, there is insufficient information to describe who or what it represents.
role: Not mentioned in the passages.

definition: Alstoozori refers to Beelzebub's "distressing" or "sorrowful reflections." These reflections were specifically concerning the lamentable fact that the results of factors perfected for normal "being-logical mentation," which were transmitted by heredity, had not been preserved in terrestrial beings.
role: Beelzebub's sorrowful reflections about the failure of terrestrial beings to preserve their logical mentation.

key_quotes.text: Well, my boy, after these distressing 'alstoozori' of mine or, as your favorites would say, 'sorrowful reflections,' I wished to clarify for myself the true cause of this lamentable fact, and as a result of careful investigations I finally understood and became aware with my whole being that this abnormality is due exclusively to one aspect of the chief particularity of their strange psyche, that particularity which has become a completely crystallized, inseparable part of their common presence and serves as a factor for the pe-riodic arising in them of the 'urgent need to destroy everything outside themselves.

definition: Amambakhlutr was a "wise and learned Asiatic being" from the "dim and distant past." Through his conscious observations, he discovered that an "oily something" accumulating in skin pores had a harmful effect on the general functioning of the planetary body. He led investigations with other learned beings, concluding that it was impossible to persuade beings to stop wearing clothes. As a result, they devised a method to artificially remove these "residues of the second being-food" from skin pores by establishing a necessary being-usage, which led to the creation of "hammams" (bathhouses). He found that ordinary washing, even with hot water, was ineffective for removing these deep oily deposits.
role: A wise and learned Asiatic being who discovered the harmful effects of oily skin deposits and initiated the establishment of hammams (bathhouses) for their removal.

key_quotes.text: Well, my boy, in the 'dim and distant past,' as your contemporary favorites say, a wise and learned Asiatic being by the name of Amambakhlutr once clearly noticed, in the course of his conscious observations of various facts proceeding around him, that this 'oily something' that collects in the pores of the skin has a maleficent influence on the general functioning of the whole planetary body, so he began to study this evil and search for a means of getting rid of it.

The result of the investigations and long deliberations of this Amambakhlutr, and of several other learned beings who became his followers and helpers, was that they became convinced of the impossibility of persuading beings similar to themselves not to wear clothes, and decided to find a means for artificially eliminating these residues of the second being-food from the pores of the skin by implanting in the psyche of the beings around them some being-usage or other which would become indispensable in the course of time, and in this way would enter into their habits and customs

And what these learned Asiatic beings with the great Amambakhlutr at their head experimentally elucidated and put into practice, well, this was the origin of those 'hammams' which exist there in various places even till now.

definition: Amarkhoodan is described as the sacred 'third being-food' by the beings of the continent of Atlantis. Its name signified for them 'help for God'. The conscious absorption of this and other higher sacred cosmic substances was considered necessary for the arising and existence of higher being-parts and for the 'striving for self-perfection'. The absence of a conscious need for absorbing these substances led to the disappearance of this striving and the capacity for 'intentional contemplation'.
role: A sacred 'third being-food' whose conscious absorption was essential for the development of higher being-parts and self-perfection.

key_quotes.text: The beings of the continent of Atlantis then named the second being-food 'amarloos,' which meant 'help for the moon,' and they named the third being-food the sacred 'amarkhoodan,' which signified for them 'help for God.'

The fact is that when they ceased to absorb intentionally the cosmic substances necessary for the arising and existence of the higher being-parts, the very striving for self-perfection disappeared from their common presence, as well as the capacity for what is called 'intentional contemplation,' which is the principal factor for the assimilation of those sacred cosmic substances

definition: Amarloos is described as a sacred 'being-duty' for the beings of the continent of Atlantis, specifically during the period of the 'Samliosian civilization'. In their language, it meant 'help for the Moon'. It is also referred to as the 'second being-food' by the beings of Atlantis. This being-duty, alongside the duty of perfecting their higher bodies and serving the Most Great Cosmic Trogoautoegocrat, was scrupulously practiced through specific customs.
role: A sacred 'being-duty' and 'second being-food' for Atlanteans, meaning 'help for the Moon'.

key_quotes.text: this being-duty as sacred and called it 'amarloos,' which in their language meant 'help for the Moon. '

And so the beings of that period, which was then called the 'Samliosian civilization,' devised and very scrupulously practiced certain customs that contributed to the fulfillment of those being-duties in the most effective way.

The beings of the continent of Atlantis then named the second being-food 'amarloos,' which meant 'help for the moon,' and they named the third being-food the sacred 'amarkhoodan,' which signified for them 'help for God.'

definition: 'Anoklinism' is identified as one of several 'definite new teachings' that appeared as new forms of 'being-kalkali' (essential strivings) in the psyche of Earth's ordinary beings. These teachings arose due to a 'maleficent' branch of science and resulted in the disappearance of essential data like 'patriarchality' and 'religious feeling' in beings, leading to further derangement of their psyche.
role: A teaching that, as an 'essential striving' ('being-kalkali'), contributed to the psychological derangement and loss of fundamental impulses in Earth beings.

key_quotes.text: Thanks merely to this branch of their science, there were acquired in the psyche of the ordinary beings of this ill-fated planet several new forms of what are called 'being-kalkali' —that is, 'essential strivings'—which appeared in the guise of certain definite new teachings existing under the names of 'anoklinism,' 'Darwinism,' 'Anthroposophism,' 'Theosophism,' and many others with names also ending in 'ism,' with the result that even those two data in their presence, which still helped them to be at least a little as three-centered beings should be, have finally disappeared in them.

This branch of their contemporary science was the cause not only of the appearance of several additional and pernicious 'kalkali' in their common presence, but also of the further derangement in many of them of the abnormal functioning of their psyche, which to their great misfortune had long since been disharmonized to the degree of 'alnokoorian cacophony.'

definition: 'Anoroparionikima' was one of three special, imposing buildings constructed in every region and district on the continent of Atlantis. This specific building was reserved for beings referred to as the 'middle sex'. Additionally, beings with certain 'symptoms' (described as an illness, 'hemorrhoids', by contemporary beings) were obliged to stay in these buildings during indicated periods. Their confinement was to prevent their 'taints' and 'emanations' from interfering with the quiet and regular existence of 'normal beings', though they were not compelled to perform any specific actions within. These buildings were part of a system for fulfilling being-duties.
role: A special building on Atlantis designed for beings of the 'middle sex' and for the confinement of certain individuals whose 'emanations' were deemed disruptive to normal society.

key_quotes.text: The third, for beings then called the 'middle sex,' was called 'anoroparionikima.'

Beings with these symptoms were obliged to remain in these anoroparionikimas during the periods indicated by the beings around them, but they were under no compulsion to do anything, and existed as they pleased In regard to them there was only one purpose, which was that they should neither meet nor speak with normal beings of the given locality.

They were confined in these buildings because at certain periods of the month, according to the prevailing ideas, on account of their various 'taints,' their 'emanations' interfered with the quiet and regular existence of beings surrounding them.
In every region, and even in the separate districts of the regions, three special, very imposing buildings were required to be built.

definition: The common-cosmic Ansanbaluiazar is defined as the aggregate of the results issuing from all cosmic sources, both great and small. It is actualized by the laws of vibrations from all sources. Objective Science describes it with the formulation: 'Everything issuing from everything and again entering into everything.'
role: An aggregate of results from all cosmic sources.

key_quotes.text: impossible for me by means of this contemporary sound-producing instrument, the piano, which I brought back from the surface of your planet, to give you a full explanation of the laws of vibrations from all sources which actualize the common-cosmic Ansanbaluiazar, as could have been done so ideally on the remarkable lav-mertz-nokh
And the aggregate of the results issuing from all cosmic sources, great and small, they then named the 'common-cosmic Ansanbaluiazar.'

It is interesting to remark concerning this 'common-cosmic Ansanbaluiazar' that present-day Objective Science has the formulation.' 'Everything issuing from everything and again entering into everything.

definition: Antkooano is described as a sacred process that can only take place on planets where all cosmic truths have become known to all the beings there. This sacred process is intentionally actualized by the All-Foreseeing Common Endless Father in three-brained beings. During its action in their presence, the excess of the 'Holy Reconciling' (the third holy force of the fundamental holy cosmic law of Triamazikamno), which is obtained during the assimilation of cosmic truths, crystallizes in them the data for engendering 'egoaitoorassian being-will.' The perfecting of Objective Reason can proceed only from personal conscious labor and intentional suffering, which is linked to this process.
role: A sacred process for the perfecting of three-brained beings.

key_quotes.text: This sacred 'antkooano' can take place only on those planets on which all cosmic truths have become known to all the beings there.

Thanks to this sacred process, intentionally actualized by our All-Foreseeing Common Endless Father in the three-brained beings of these planets, it has been foreseen that during the action in their presence of the fundamental holy cosmic law of Triamazikamno, the excess of its third holy force, namely, the 'Holy Reconciling,' obtained during the assimilation of cosmic truths of that order, should by itself crystallize in them the data for engendering that 'something' which is called 'egoaitoorassian being-will. '
the perfecting of Objective Reason can proceed only from personal conscious labor and intentional suffering.

definition: Arch-Cherub Helkgematios is identified as the All-Quarters Maintainer and Great Arch-Cherub. He is also the governor of the holy planet, Purgatory, and at his own wish, he undertook its chief organization and government. Helkgematios was the first being, after the creation of the world, to merit the sacred Anklad, meaning he attained the highest degree of Reason that an independent Individual can reach, which is three degrees below the Absolute Reason of Endlessness. He sanctioned the choice of fifty righteous souls for perfecting and expressed a desire to help them.
role: Governor of the holy planet Purgatory and an All-Quarters Maintainer with a very high degree of Reason.

key_quotes.text: His All-Quarters Maintainer, the Arch-Cherub Helkgematios, the governor of the holy planet, not only sanctioned the choice of these fifty blessed souls but, by his own all-gracious decision, expressed the desire to help them in every way in fulfilling their undertaking.

It was then that for the first time this holy planet received the name of 'Purgatory,' and its chief organization and government were undertaken at his own wish by our All-Quarters Maintainer, the Great Arch-Cherub Helkgematios, that same great Helkgematios who after the creation of the world first merited the sacred Anklad, that is, first attained that degree of Reason which is the highest that can be attained by an independent Individual, whatever his nature, and which is the third degree below the Absolute Reason of our Endlessness.

definition: Arch-Cunning was a nickname given to King Appolis by his subjects, who used it behind his back. King Appolis was known for employing various and reasonable methods of 'intimidation' and 'threat' to extract what was needed from his subjects to maintain the greatness of his community. Despite his severity, his subjects could not help respecting him due to his methods.
role: A nickname for King Appolis, known for his clever and respected methods of rule.

key_quotes.text: His methods were so varied, and at the same time so reasonable, that even those of his subjects in whom these consequences had already been crystallized could not help respecting him, although they nicknamed him, of course behind his back, the 'Arch-Cunning.'

definition: Arguenia is described as a small town on the planet Earth, which in an older era was considered the most remote point of the country of Maralpleicie. It was inhabited by a considerable number of beings belonging to the second Asiatic group, whose primary activity was the extraction of 'turquoise' from nature. Beelzebub visited the 'chaikhanas' in this town as part of his observations.
role: A small, remote town on Earth where turquoise was extracted.

key_quotes.text: We finally arrived at a small town called 'Arguenia,' which in those days was considered the most remote point of the country of Maralpleicie.
It was inhabited by a fair number of beings of this second Asiatic group, who were engaged chiefly in extracting from nature what is called 'turquoise.'
In this small town of Arguenia, in accordance with my usual procedure, I began to visit various 'chaikhanas,' pursuing there, as always, the fulfillment of my principal aim.

definition: Ash-hark (also spelled Ashhark) is a continent. According to a doctrine, souls on this continent that have been idle, indolent, negligent, existing for self-gratification, or not keeping commandments are sent to a smaller neighboring island for further existence, while meritorious souls go to 'Paradise'. Many 'spirits' attendant upon 'Mister God' exist on this continent, observing beings unnoticed. Ash-hark and other lands were created to serve 'Mister God' and the meritorious souls on His island, serving as places of preparation and storehouses. It was also described as the chief center of existence for three-brained beings at a certain period. The text explicitly states that the continent of Ashhark is now called 'Asia'.
role: A continent, a chief center of existence for three-brained beings, later identified as Asia.

key_quotes.text: If the 'soul' has fulfilled its duties honestly and conscientiously, Mister God leaves it to continue its existence on His island, but the 'soul' that here on the continent of Ash-hark has been idle or discharged its duties indolently or negligently, existing only for the gratification of the desires of its attached parts, or finally, has not kept His commandments, such a soul our Mister God sends to a smaller neighboring island for its further existence.

Here on the continent of Ashhark exist many 'spirits' attendant upon Him, who walk among us wearing 'caps of invisibility,' thanks to which they can constantly watch us unnoticed and report all our doings to Mister God, or inform Him on the 'Day of Judgment ' We can conceal nothing from them, neither our actions nor our thoughts.

This doctrine also said that our continent of Ashhark and all the other continents and islands of the world were created by our Mister God only to serve Him and the meritorious 'souls' already dwelling on His island All the continents and islands of the world are, as it were, places of preparation and storehouses for everything needed for this island of His.
This sea was situated in the northwestern part of the great, newly arisen continent of Ashhark, which at that period was the chief center of existence of the three-brained beings there.
For example, the continent of Ashhark is now called 'Asia';

Specific geographical features like the Colhidius (Caspian) Sea and the Oxoseria (Amu Darya) river flowed into or through parts of Ashhark, with the river later forming the 'Aral Sea' on its surface. Cities such as Koorkalai (capital of Tikliamish), Mosulopolis, and Kronbookhon (capital of Nievia) are mentioned as being on this continent (Asia), and it is a place where activities like the diminishing of 'increase' and the birth of significant beings like Atarnakh and Lentrohamsanin occurred.

The other shores of this sea belonged to those newly emerged land masses that had become joined to the continent of Ashhark, and taken together were at first known as 'Frianktzanaralis' and a little later as 'Colhidchissi.'

Here it might as well be added that although this large river, then called the 'Oxoseria,' still exists today, it no longer flows into the Caspian Sea because, as a result of a minor planetary tremor, it was diverted to the north about halfway down its course and flowed into one of the hollows on the surface of the continent of Ashhark, where it gradually formed a small sea still known as the 'Aral Sea.'

definition: Ashiata Shiemash, referred to as 'Very Saintly' and later 'Most Saintly' and 'Essence-Loving,' was a unique Messenger sent from Above to the planet Earth. His mission was to help three-brained beings free themselves from the consequences of a particular implanted organ. He notably refused to use the customary methods of previous Messengers, opting instead to not teach or preach directly to ordinary beings. Through his holy labors, he succeeded in creating conditions on Earth where the existence of its unfortunate beings temporarily resembled that of three-brained beings on other planets. Information about his saintly activities and deliberations, including a 'legomonism' titled 'The Terror of the Situation,' was transmitted through 'initiates.' He also left a preserved 'marble tablet' engraved with his 'counsels' and 'commandments,' which became a sacred relic. He intentionally created 'new forms of existence' that later disappeared due to the destruction of his labors by the beings themselves. He pondered on Mount Vezniama to plan his activities, then went to Djoolfapal, where he engaged with the Tchaftantoori Brotherhood, enlightening their Reason and guiding their being-impulses. He renewed 'all the principles of Being of the initiated beings,' which were subsequently called 'Ashiata's renewals,' and explained the 'being-impulse of Objective Conscience' to 'great initiates.' His beneficent efforts were largely destroyed, with a 'learned being of new formation' named Lentrohamsanin identified as a key culprit. He was born in the Sumerian race, in the village of Pispascana near Babylon, and his 'highest being-part' became 'Omnicosmic' during his self-preparation.
role: A unique, very saintly Messenger from Above to Earth, who worked to improve the state of three-brained beings through intentional creations and the enlightenment of initiates.

key_quotes.text: The Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash was the only Messenger sent from Above to your planet who by his holy labors succeeded in creating conditions in which for a certain time the existence of its unfortunate beings somewhat resembled the existence of three-brained beings with the same possibilities that inhabit other planets of our Great Universe And this saint was also the first who, for the accomplishment of the mission assigned to him, refused to employ the customary methods established during centuries by all the other Messengers from Above for the three-brained beings of that planet.

The Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash taught nothing whatever to the ordinary three-brained beings of the Earth, nor did he preach anything to them, as was done before and after him by all the other Messengers sent from Above with the same aim.

Definite information relating to his very saintly activities did, however, pass from the contemporaries of the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash to the beings of following generations through those known as 'initiates,' by means of a certain 'legomonism' of his deliberations under the title of 'The Terror of the Situation.'

In addition to this, there has been preserved from the period of his very saintly activities, and exists even to the present day, a 'marble tablet' on which were engraved his 'counsels' and 'commandments' to the beings of that time.

...new forms of existence, intentionally created by Ashiata Shiemash himself, began to be fixed and to flow automatically in the process of their being-existence, these strange psychic properties entirely disappeared from the presence of most of the three-brained beings there Later, however, when they themselves destroyed all the results of the very saintly labors of this Essence-Loving Ashiata Shiemash,

My research and investigations also made clear to me that after the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash had pondered on Mount Vezniama and had drawn up in his mind a definite plan for his further most saintly activities, he did not return to the city of Babylon but went straight to the city of 'Djoolfapal,' the capital of a country then called 'Kurlandtech' situated in the middle of the continent of Asia.
...all the principles of Being of the initiated beings there were renewed by the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash and later came to be called 'Ashiata's renewals. '

The basis of all the great and small maleficent activities of those 'learned' beings and of all their unconscious harmful manifestations leading to the destruction of the last remnants of the results, so beneficent for the three-brained beings there, of the very saintly conscious labors of the Essence-Loving Ashiata Shiemash was, as my further detailed research on his saintly activities showed me, the 'invention' of a learned being, well known in his day, who was also a 'learned being of new formation,' named Lentrohamsanin.
He was born in that part of the continent of Asia which is called the 'Sumerian race,' in a small village called 'Pispascana," situated not far from Babylon.

definition: Ashiata's renewals refer to 'all the principles of Being of the initiated beings' on the planet Earth that were renewed by the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash.
role: Renewed principles of Being for initiated beings on Earth.

key_quotes.text: You should note that at that time all the principles of Being of the initiated beings there were renewed by the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash and later came to be called 'Ashiata's renewals. '

definition: Ashiatan refers to an 'epoch' or period in time, specifically mentioned in the context of principles of initiation that existed 'before' it. This implies it signifies the era associated with the arrival and influence of the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash.
role: An epoch or period, likely associated with Ashiata Shiemash.

key_quotes.text: This brotherhood was founded five of their years before the arrival of the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash on the initiative of two terrestrial three-brained beings who had become genuine 'initiates' according to the principles existing before the 'Ashiatan' epoch.

definition: Askokin is a sacred cosmic substance that is deemed indispensable for the maintenance of the Moon and Anulios, which are identified as former parts of the planet Earth. This substance is required to issue continuously from Earth. Throughout the Universe, 'askokin' generally exists blended with other sacred substances named 'abrustdonis' and 'helkdonis'. For 'askokin' to possess the degree of vivifyingness necessary for cosmic maintenance, it must first be separated from 'abrustdonis' and 'helkdonis'. This separation process occurs when three-brained beings, on any planet, transmute these latter two sacred substances within themselves for the purpose of forming and perfecting their higher being-bodies, specifically the 'kesdjan body' and the 'body of the soul,' through conscious labor and intentional suffering. The text also states that a surplus of this sacred 'askokin' can arise from the planet Earth due to vibrations produced during the process of 'rascooarno' (destruction of existence) of beings of every exterior form. This surplus is described as seriously hindering the correct exchange of substances between the Moon and its atmosphere, potentially leading to an abnormally formed atmosphere and becoming an obstacle to the harmonious movement of the entire System Ors, with the danger of causing a great cosmic catastrophe.
role: A sacred cosmic substance essential for the maintenance of cosmic bodies like the Moon and Anulios, whose proper formation and transformation on Earth are linked to the spiritual efforts and physical processes of beings.

key_quotes.text: been categorically decided by the Most High and Most Sacred Individuals to take the necessary measures to insure the formation on the Earth of what is called the sacred 'askokin,' so that this sacred cosmic substance, indispensable for the maintenance of the Moon and Anulios, might issue continuously from your planet.

This cosmic substance, the sacred 'askokin,' exists throughout the Universe, generally blended with the sacred substances 'abrustdonis' and 'helkdonis,' and therefore, in order to have the degree of vivifyingness required for such maintenance, the sacred substance 'askokin' must first be freed from the other two substances.

the separation of the sacred 'askokin' from the two other substances proceeds when beings, on whatever planet they may be, transmute these sacred substances in themselves for the forming and perfecting of their higher bodies, by means of conscious labor and intentional suffering.

there is already a surplus of the sacred "askokin" required from the planet Earth for the maintenance of its former parts, a surplus, that is, of those vibrations that arise during the process of "rascooarno" of beings of every exterior form on that planet from which this sacred cosmic substance is required.

This surplus of the sacred "askokin" is already seriously hindering the correct exchange of substances between the Moon and its atmosphere, with the danger that the atmosphere may in consequence be formed abnormally, and later become an obstacle to the harmonious movement of the whole system Ors, and perhaps again give rise to yet another catastrophe on a great cosmic scale.

definition: Assadulla Ibrahim Ogly was an ingenious and energetic Persian dervish who primarily traveled across the continent of Asia, focusing on countries where most three-brained beings followed Islam. He mainly operated through other dervishes, whom he very cleverly convinced of his idea. These dervishes, in turn, persuaded the ordinary beings of Asia that the destruction of other forms of existence was displeasing to God, and that those who committed such acts would suffer a double punishment in hell, both for their own sins and for the sins of the beings they had slaughtered. As a result of this preaching, the beings of Asia reduced their sacrificial offerings year by year. The text states that the 'final result of all the activity of this humane Persian dervish' was the last great process of reciprocal destruction, known by terrestrial beings as 'the First World War'.
role: An ingenious and energetic Persian dervish whose efforts to reduce sacrificial offerings in Asia ultimately led to unforeseen negative consequences on a global scale.

key_quotes.text: Everywhere he went, this ingenious and energetic Persian, Assadulla Ibrahim Ogly, very cleverly persuaded these other dervishes of the 'truth' of his idea, and these in their turn persuaded the ordinary beings of Asia not only that the destruction of the existence of beings of other forms is dis-pleasing to God, but that those who destroyed them would even be obliged to suffer a double punishment 'in another world' in hell, both for their own 'sins' and for the 'sins' of the beings they had slaughtered, and so on.

Thanks to this kind of preaching about the 'other world' by dervishes, considered great authorities in such matters, the beings of Asia did indeed, year by year, reduce the number of their sacrificial offerings.
And the final result of all the activity of this 'humane Persian dervish' was precisely the last great process of reciprocal destruction or, as your favorites call it, 'the First World War. '

definition: Assooparatsata is another name for the "Milky Way," described as a cosmic space. The trans-space communication ship Karnak is mentioned as having left the spaces of the "Assooparatsata" (the Milky Way) during its voyage.
role: A name for the Milky Way galaxy.

key_quotes.text: Having left the spaces of the "Assooparatsata," that is, the "Milky Way," it was flying from the planet "Karatas" to the solar system "Pandetznokh," the sun of which is called the "Pole Star. "

definition: Astralnomian-Deuteroëhary is a specific cosmic substance formed through the transmutation of 'astralnomian-protoëhary'. This transformation occurs within beings when 'astralnomian-protoëhary' mixes with the substances of the "first being-food" that have already evolved up to the third stopinder of the sacred being-Heptaparaparshinokh. As these mixed substances further evolve together, they not only help the "first being-food" to be transmuted into 'being-tetartoëhary,' but the 'astralnomian-protoëhary' itself is transmuted into 'astralnomian-deuteroëhary'.
role: A transmuted cosmic substance resulting from the evolution and mixing of 'astralnomian-protoëhary' with the "first being-food" substances within beings.

key_quotes.text: And then the substances of this 'astralnomianprotoëhary,' entering into the presence of beings for their own evolution and still having in themselves, according to the Sacred Heptaparaparshinokh, all the possibilities of evolving from their centers of gravity by the process of harnel-miatznel alone, mix with the totality of substances of the first being-food which have already evolved up to the third stopinder of the sacred being-Heptaparaparshinokh, and further evolve together, and thus help these substances of the first being-food to pass through the lower 'mechano-coinciding mdnel-in' and to be transmuted into other definite substances, named 'being-tetartoëhary,' while the 'astralnomian-protoëhary' itself is transmuted into the substances named 'astralnomian-deuteroëhary. '

definition: Astralnomian-Protoëhary is a type of 'protoëhary' that is formed from the active elements of the "second being-food," which is equated with "air." These active elements enter the presence of beings through the nose and, through various local 'harnel-miatznel' processes, evolve and are transformed in what are called the "lungs" into this 'astralnomian-protoëhary'. This substance then enters the beings' presence for its own evolution, carrying within it the possibilities of evolving from its centers of gravity through the process of 'harnel-miatznel' alone. Subsequently, it mixes with the totality of substances of the "first being-food" (which have already evolved to the third stopinder) and further evolves together with them, assisting the "first being-food" substances in their transmutation into 'being-tetartoëhary'. During this combined evolutionary process, 'astralnomian-protoëhary' itself is transmuted into 'astralnomian-deuteroëhary'.
role: A cosmic substance formed from "air" within beings, serving as an intermediate stage in a larger process of substance transformation and evolution, eventually transforming into 'astralnomian-deuteroëhary'.

key_quotes.text: The active elements that compose this 'second being-food,' or 'air,' enter the presence of beings through the nose and, gradually evolving with the cooperation of various processes of harnel-miatznel of local character, are transformed in what are called the 'lungs' into 'protoëhary,' but this time into 'astralnomian-protoëhary. '

And then the substances of this 'astralnomianprotoëhary,' entering into the presence of beings for their own evolution and still having in themselves, according to the Sacred Heptaparaparshinokh, all the possibilities of evolving from their centers of gravity by the process of harnel-miatznel alone, mix with the totality of substances of the first being-food which have already evolved up to the third stopinder of the sacred being-Heptaparaparshinokh, and further evolve together, and thus help these substances of the first being-food to pass through the lower 'mechano-coinciding mdnel-in' and to be transmuted into other definite substances, named 'being-tetartoëhary,' while the 'astralnomian-protoëhary' itself is transmuted into the substances named 'astralnomian-deuteroëhary. '

definition: Atarnakh was a renowned philosopher and learned being from the city of Mosulopolis on the planet Earth, born into a Kurdish family. He gained fame for his theory, detailed in a treatise titled 'Why Do Wars Occur on the Earth?', which he developed after years of studying the fundamental question of "What is in general the sense and meaning of man's existence?" His investigations led him to an ancient Sumerian manuscript containing a supposition about a "law of the reciprocal maintenance of everything existing," which became the cornerstone of his theory. His theory proposed that a law of "reciprocal maintenance of everything that exists" is facilitated by certain chemical substances, which enable the "spiritualization of beings" (life) and serve the maintenance of existence after a being's death. He also logically argued that a specific quantity of deaths, producing vibrations of a particular "degree of power," must periodically occur on Earth, independently of human will. The text notes that his theory was "close to reality" and that his hypotheses were very similar to the great fundamental cosmic law, Trogoautoegocrat. Despite his profound insights, he failed to understand the crucial qualitative significance of the vibrations formed during the process of 'rascooarno', focusing instead on quantitative aspects. His ideas caused considerable division within a learned society, ultimately leading to its dissolution after its members, upon his direction, concluded that wars were an inevitable natural occurrence. He was characterized as truly learned but also very proud and self-loving.
role: A famous, learned Earth philosopher of Kurdish origin whose theory on the inevitable nature of wars, though close to cosmic truth, missed a crucial qualitative aspect and greatly influenced a learned society.

key_quotes.text: All the following events came about through the influence of a famous philosopher, named Atarnakh, and of the theory expounded by him in a treatise under the title 'Why Do Wars Occur on the Earth?'
This Atarnakh was born in that same city of Mosulopolis, into a family of what are called 'Kurds.'

The idea expressed in the ancient manuscript so captivated the philosopher Atarnakh that from then on he devoted himself entirely to the study of this one aspect of the question that had interested him.

All the hypotheses of this Kurd, Atarnakh, were very similar to the essence of the great fundamental cosmic law, Trogoautoegocrat, existing in our Universe, which I explained to you more or less in detail when I was speaking about the Holy Planet Purgatory

It was very definitely proved in this theory of the philosopher Atarnakh that there must inevitably exist in the world a law of the 'reciprocal maintenance of everything that exists,' and that this 'reciprocal maintenance' is assisted by certain chemical substances through which the process of the spiritualization of beings, that is to say, 'life,' takes place, and these chemical substances serve the maintenance of all that exists only after the life of a being ceases, that is, when a being dies.

It was only after this categorical resolution was passed, and all the members of that serious society had unanimously decided to begin that very day liquidating all their affairs, that the Kurd, Atarnakh, who although considered there as truly learned was nonetheless very proud and self-loving, ascended the rostrum and spoke as follows: 'My honorable colleagues

although, as I have already told you, the hypothesis put forward by that unusual learned Kurd, Atarnakh, came very close to the truth, he failed to understand the most important thing, which is that the vibrations re-quired by Great Nature that are formed from the emanations issuing from the process of their 'rascooarno' have no significance quantitatively, but only qualitatively.

definition: Autoegocrat refers to the former system responsible for maintaining the existence of the Sun Absolute. It is explicitly contrasted with the later "trogoautoegocratic" system, which was established at the time of the creation of the Megalocosmos. The text suggests that the transformation of substances through "beings-apparatuses" was to proceed according to a certain law or system associated with it.
role: A former cosmic system for maintaining the Sun Absolute, later replaced by the Trogoautoegocratic system.

key_quotes.text: So, my boy, at this point I can give you one more illustration of the difference between Autoegocrat and Trogoautoegocrat, that is, of the difference between the former 'autoegocratic' system of maintaining the existence of the Sun Absolute and the 'trogoautoegocratic' system established at the creation of the Megalocosmos.

definition: Avazlin was the former name of a district on Earth where great artifacts, including a new observatory and other buildings for the welfare of being-existence, were being constructed. A few years after being called Avazlin, this district came to be known as 'Caironana,' and is currently referred to as the 'outskirts of Cairo.'
role: A former name for a district on Earth, now called the outskirts of Cairo.

key_quotes.text: The district where the work was being carried on—both for this new 'observatory,' as they called it, and for other buildings designed for the welfare of their being-existence —was then named 'Avazlin'; a few years later it came to be called 'Caironana,' and today it is referred to simply as the 'outskirts of Cairo.'

definition: Being-data refers to required information or factors that can be crystallized or decrystallized within the common presence of beings. They are necessary for understanding reality, can form accurate opinions, and are described as 'essential data' that engender 'being-impulses' such as patriarchality and religious feeling, or are used for obtaining Objective Reason.
role: Information or factors that are crystallized in beings, engendering impulses and necessary for understanding or Objective Reason.

key_quotes.text: not only did the data for this 'automatic opinion' about them begin to be decrystallized in me, but there began to be crystallized the required 'being-data' for finding out how there could have been formed in the common presence of the beings of other communities an opinion that was so contrary to reality.

even those two data in their presence, which still helped them to be at least a little as three-centered beings should be, have finally disappeared in them. These essential data, which until quite recently have been present in them, engendered the being-impulses they called 'patriarchality' and 'religious feeling.'
all the data for obtaining Objective Reason

definition: Being-Diardookino is directly defined as a certain 'experiencing' within a being. This experiencing, when combined with other factors, slowly but surely gives rise to the specific character of a being's common psyche. The passages illustrate 'experiencing' through a sequence of internal events such as calmness, attracting attention, noticing a likeness, remembering a cake, enjoying food, hearing a compliment, and inwardly rejoicing.
role: An internal process of experiencing or sensation that shapes a being's psyche.

key_quotes.text: in him a certain 'being-diardookino' or, as they would say, a certain 'experiencing.' And only later the speaker, in conforming to their grammatical rule, finally pronounces their famous 'nicht,' with the result that there is produced in their common presence each time something that slowly but surely gives rise to the aforesaid specific character of their common psyche, and all this should help you to elucidate the original problem I have set you.
Do you deny that on accidentally overhearing these words, perhaps said out loud for your benefit, the whole of you, as is said, 'inwardly rejoices'?

definition: Being-Duties are responsibilities or tasks that beings must carry out. One specific, albeit negative, example given for Earth beings is the 'process of 'reciprocal destruction''. Beelzebub also speaks of being 'free from any other being-duties' when undertaking another task, implying a broader category of obligations. The text also details 'being-obligolnian strivings'—five fundamental purposes for working towards genuine Conscience and self-perfection, which, while called 'strivings', function as inherent obligations for beings.
role: Obligations or responsibilities that beings must fulfill, sometimes encompassing fundamental strivings for self-perfection or specific destructive actions.

key_quotes.text: offering them, as they say, 'philanthropic aid,' especially as regards their first 'being-duty,' namely, that of carrying out from time to time the process of 'reciprocal destruction.'
Thanks to them, the discharge of that 'being-duty' has gradually become, for your contemporary favorites, the 'merest trifle.'

When I returned to our dear Karatas, and was free from any other being-duties, I voluntarily took upon myself the responsible guidance of your 'oskiano' or, as your favorites would say, your 'education for the being of a responsible being.'

All the beings of that planet then began to work in order to have in their consciousness this divine function of genuine Conscience, and for this purpose, as everywhere in the Universe, they transubstantiated in themselves what are called the 'being-obligolnian strivings' of which there are five...

definition: Being-Exioëhary is a substance, explicitly equated with 'sperm', which is formed within beings. Knowledge regarding its origin and significance is closely connected to the fundamental, sacred cosmic law of Heptaparaparshinokh. It was understood to be a means by which beings could perfect themselves, though specific instructions or methods for achieving this were unfortunately lost or not fully known.
role: A substance (sperm) within beings that is a means for self-perfection, linked to cosmic law.

key_quotes.text: concerning the question of 'being-exioëhary,' as this will give you additional data for elucidating by a concrete example certain particularities that I have already indicated of the fundamental, sacred cosmic law of Heptaparaparshinokh.

In this fragmentary, yet authentic information, it was very convincingly indicated that by means of the substance 'exioëhary,' or 'sperm,' formed in them, it was possible to perfect oneself, but unfortunately for them they found no indications in these surviving fragments of what had to be done and precisely how it was to be done.

definition: Being-Heptaparaparshinokh is a 'primordial sacred law' of the cosmos that governs the actualization of processes in both the greatest and smallest cosmic concentrations. It functions with 'stopinders' that have subjective actions and possesses 'evolutionary and involutionary properties.' This law dictates how actions at any intermediate stage of cosmic processes influence other stages. Its most important branch is the 'law of vibrations,' which provides the possibility of recognizing reality. Knowledge of this law is essential for understanding other laws of 'world-creation' and 'world-existence,' and helps three-brained beings to ponder their existence and reconcile internal 'individual collisions'.
role: A fundamental, sacred cosmic law that governs universal processes, vibrations, and is key to understanding existence and other cosmic laws.

key_quotes.text: Thus from that time on, the actualization of the primordial sacred law of Heptaparaparshinokh began to proceed in the greatest as well as the smallest cosmic concentrations with the subjective action of its stopinders changed in this way.

knowledge of these two sacred laws, especially knowledge of the particularities of the sacred Heptaparaparshinokh, will help you later to understand very well and easily all the second-order and third-order laws of 'world-creation' and 'world-existence. '
the 'law of vibrations,' which is the most important branch of all and gives the possibility of recognizing reality at least

as regards the evolutionary and involutionary properties of the Law of Heptaparaparshinokh, the action at any separate intermediate stage in the total functioning of one of these results influences the action at each separate intermediate stage in another, exactly as in its own functioning, and furthermore that with respect to the properties of the vibrations of which they are constituted, these transitory cosmic results have complete affinity.

definition: The provided passages consistently refer to 'being-partkdolgduty' (singular) as an indispensable function or duty for every three-brained being. It involves the careful processing of new impressions, requiring 'sane deliberation' to verify them by confronting and evaluating them against already deposited data, rather than merely accepting them. Not fulfilling this leads to superficial perception. The 'results of partkdolgduty' are crucial for the stable duration of a being's existence, and its intensive actualization provides the 'power' to instinctively sense 'Objective Reason'. The plural form 'Being-Partkdolgduties' is not explicitly defined, but can be inferred to refer to the various aspects or instances of this singular, essential function.
role: A core, indispensable duty or function in beings related to discerning reality, sustaining existence, and perceiving objective truth.

key_quotes.text: gradually allowed the other parts of their total presence to perceive every new impression without fulfilling what is called 'being-partkdolgduty,' that is to say, merely as such impressions are generally perceived by one or another of their independent localizations known as 'being-centers.'

Or, to put it in their language, they believe everything anybody says, instead of believing only what they have been able to verify by their own 'sane deliberation'—in other words, only those convictions they have reached as a result of confronting and evaluating the data already deposited in them, which have given rise to different conceptions in each of their localizations of diverse nature.

when the results of partkdolgduty were no longer obtained in the presence of your favorites, and the duration of their existence began to depend exclusively on the results of the mentioned seven accidental exterior factors, then thanks to all this the length of their existence, especially among contemporary beings, became extremely variable.

psychic factors for the power to intensively actualize being-partkdolgduty indispensable to every three-brained being— had until now been so blind that he was unable even instinctively to sense that Uni-Being and unshakable reality beneath an exterior coated in conformity with the common-cosmic Trogoautoegocrat and surrounding conditions—a reality sacred for every breathing creature, which bears the name of 'Objective Reason. '

definition: Being-Pity is described as a 'genuine impulse' or feeling that arises in a being for the 'infinitely unhappy fate' of others, particularly when their suffering is caused by circumstances that are hardly dependent on themselves. Beelzebub noted that he scarcely felt this impulse for the three-brained beings of Earth until later in his observations of them.
role: A genuine impulse of compassion or sorrow felt for the suffering of others, particularly when their fate is beyond their control.

key_quotes.text: I scarcely ever felt in my common presence a genuine impulse of being-pity for the infinitely unhappy fate of these favorites of yours, caused by circumstances hardly depending on themselves at all.
