definition: A large city on the continent of Ashhark, serving as the capital for the second group of three-brained beings there. It was situated on the southeastern shore of the 'Sea of Beneficence' and was renowned across the planet for its production of fine 'fabrics' and 'precious ornaments'. The city spanned both banks of the mouth of the 'Keria Chi' river, which flowed from the eastern highlands into the Sea of Beneficence, and was also near another large river, the 'Naria Chi'. King Koniutsion ruled over this community and tried to address the issue of poppy seed chewing among its inhabitants.
role: A significant ancient city on the continent of Ashhark, known for trade and as a center for a community of three-brained beings.

key_quotes.text: I wished to go to the capital of the beings of the second group on the continent of Ashhark, the city of 'Gob,' which was situated on the southeastern shore of that sea.
The city of Gob was then a large city, and was renowned over the whole planet for its production of fine 'fabrics' and what are called 'precious ornaments.'
The city spread over both banks of the mouth of a large river, called the 'Keria Chi,' which rose in the eastern highlands of the country and emptied into the Sea of Beneficence.
The number of those who chewed poppy seeds rose steadily in the city of Gob, and reports from other territories subject to him showed a daily increase.
When the wise King Koniutsion finally became convinced that he could not root out the passion of chewing 'gulgulian' seeds through fear of punishment...

definition: A country on the continent of Ashhark where the second independent group of three-brained beings dwelt. Its central place of existence was the city of 'Gob', and the entire country was known by the name 'Goblandia' for a long period. This region was subsequently covered by 'kashmanoonoms' (sands), and its main part is now referred to simply as the 'Gobi Desert'.
role: An ancient country on Earth, which later became the Gobi Desert.

key_quotes.text: Later, when this second group established a central place of existence, they called it the city of 'Gob,' and for a long time the whole country was called 'Goblandia.'
This region in its turn was covered by 'kashmanoonoms,' and today the chief part of this once flourishing country is known simply as the 'Gobi Desert.'

definition: A word used by the three-brained beings of the planet Saturn as a courtesy title. It is placed before the name of the individual being addressed, similar to the custom on Earth where 'mister' or other phrases are used.
role: A courtesy title or prefix used on the planet Saturn.

key_quotes.text: the word 'gornahoor' is used by the three-brained beings of the planet Saturn as a matter of courtesy, they put it in front of the name of the one they are addressing It is the same with your favorites on the planet Earth, who have invented the custom of putting the word 'mister,' or sometimes a whole meaningless phrase, in front of the name of the person they are speaking to...

definition: Beelzebub's 'essence-friend' who resides on the planet Saturn. He is described as being very learned and is a notable inventor, having created various complex apparatuses and special materials in his 'khrkh' (workshop), including a 'hrakhartzakha', 'life-chakhans' (also called 'khrikhirkhis' or 'dynamos'), and a 'solookhnorakhoona' (a complex vacuum pump). His inventions are aimed at clarifying the omnipresent cosmic substance Okidanokh. He has wings and is interested in Beelzebub's observations of the strange psyche of Earth's three-brained beings, often receiving notes from him. Beelzebub once descended to Earth at his request and attributes the fame and quality of his own observatory to Gornahoor Harharkh's knowledge. He is the 'producer' or father of Gornahoor Raoorkh.
role: Beelzebub's highly learned inventor-friend from the planet Saturn, focused on cosmic science and the study of Okidanokh.

key_quotes.text: Gornahoor Harharkh was always kept informed of my observations on their strange psyche, and that I even sent him, as well as your uncle Tooilan, copies of certain of my notes.
this future essence-friend of mine, Gornahoor Harharkh, made a sign to us to approach that special appliance he had invented, which he called 'hrakhartzakha.'
all the objects contained in this 'hrakhartzakha,' as well as everything we were to put on later, were made of special materials invented by Gornahoor Harharkh

in the enormous 'khrkh,' or workshop, of Gornahoor Harharkh, besides the 'hrakhartzakha' there were several other large independent apparatuses, and among them two quite special 'life-chakhans,' which Gornahoor Harharkh called 'khrikhirkhis.'
Gornahoor Harharkh went over to this complex pump and with his left wing moved one of its parts...
if this observatory later became famous, and was in fact the best of all the installations of its kind in the whole Universe, I am indebted chiefly to the learning of this essence-friend of mine.

definition: The 'result' or heir of Gornahoor Harharkh, and Beelzebub's 'external kesdjanian result' or 'godson', residing on the planet Saturn. He has the same exterior as his producer but is characterized as very virile and full of youthful fire, speaking with an 'angelically musical voice'. His responsible existence is devoted to the detailed study of the omnipresent cosmic substance Okidanokh, in which he has attained profound understanding, having 'smelled out its very essence'. His scientific insights led his producer, Gornahoor Harharkh, to decrystallize previous convictions and even destroy his own inventions for investigating Okidanokh, including a 'nonradiating lamp', agreeing with Raoorkh's view that his past work was an 'irredeemable sin'. Beelzebub refers to having fulfilled divine obligations towards him during a sacred ceremony called 'khri-khra-khri', similar to Earth's baptism. Gornahoor Raoorkh's scientific reply also clarified that the abnormal existence of Earth's beings was negatively affecting the normal existence and conscious self-perfecting of beings on Mars.
role: Gornahoor Harharkh's son and heir, and Beelzebub's godson, a brilliant young scientist who profoundly studies and understands the cosmic substance Okidanokh.

key_quotes.text: when just at that moment his 'result,' Gornahoor Raoorkh himself, entered the room 'Although the newcomer had exactly the same exterior as his producer, he was very virile and full of youthful fire.

Although you are only my 'kesdjanian father,' yet in view of the fact that you have fulfilled with such full awareness the divine obligations toward me taken upon yourself during my 'khri-khra-khri,' there have been crystallized in my common presence with respect to you data equivalent to those which should be in the common presence of every three-brained being toward his own producer...
my dear 'external kesdjanian result,' or 'godson,' Gornahoor Raoorkh, began to take part in our conversation.

I asked my godson, among other things, the reason for the crystallizing in his presence of data for engendering his serious interest in elucidating all aspects of the omnipresent cosmic substance Okidanokh, thanks to which he had become worthy, like his producer, of making great cosmic discoveries

Raoorkh's existence was proceeding quite well, and that he had already become his heir in all respects, having even made the aim of his responsible existence, as he himself had done, the detailed study of the omnipresent substance Okidanokh.

thanks to the results of his heir's scientific attainments, not only had all the factors for every conviction previously crystallized in his own being through long years of persevering labors now been totally decrystallized, but he had even destroyed all his inventions for investigating this omnipresent cosmic substance, including his famous 'nonradiating lamp'...

definition: A continent, which is now known as 'Africa'. It was previously a smaller continent, situated near Atlantis. During a 'terrifying perturbation' or 'second transapalnian perturbation'—a major catastrophe on the planet—a specific part of Grabontzi was identified by a tribal pythoness as a safe refuge, saving members of Beelzebub's tribe who migrated there. As a result of this perturbation, Grabontzi became significantly larger, with other land masses emerging from the waters and joining it, while some parts were engulfed and others rose, shaping it into the continent known today. A copy of the 'boolmarshano' was located on a part of its surface that was spared during this catastrophe.
role: An ancient continent on Earth, which is now known as Africa, notable for its survival and transformation during planetary catastrophes and as a refuge for Beelzebub's tribe.

key_quotes.text: a small continent named 'Sinkraga,' which lay not far from the continent of Grabontzi.
As for the continent of Grabontzi or, as it is now called, 'Africa,' it did not entirely disappear within the depths of the planet, but suffered the same fate as other still existing continents...

This smaller continent was then called 'Grabontzi' and the part the 'pythoness' indicated did indeed escape the terrifying perturbation that occurred to all the other parts of the common presence of that ill-fated planet.

As a consequence of this perturbation the continent of Grabontzi, existing today under the name of 'Africa,' became much larger, as other land masses emerged from the water spaces of the planet and were added to it.
because this copy was on a part of the surface of the continent of Grabontzi that happened to be spared, it did not enter within the planet.

definition: Holy Prana is the name given to "the Great All-Embracing of all things embraced," which is described as "the sense and striving of everything that exists in the entire Universe." It is a "most sacred particle of the Great All-Embracing of everything existing." According to Saint Buddha's actual explanations, if this most sacred Prana is crystallized in three-centered beings with the participation of their 'I', it must be perfected to a required degree of divine Reason; otherwise, this "most holy coating" will suffer and languish eternally. When perfected, these particles form a definite unit in which divine Objective Reason can become concentrated and fixed, eventually reblending with the Prime Source, spiritualized by divine Reason. However, due to a misunderstanding, Beelzebub notes that later generations, and an invention he spread, believed that this sacred Prana is already present in all beings from the moment of their arising without fulfilling any being-duty.
role: A cosmic principle or sacred particle that forms the foundation of existence and can be perfected in three-centered beings to manifest divine Objective Reason.

key_quotes.text: And this Great All-Embracing of all things embraced is called "Holy Prana." '

'You, three-centered beings of the planet Earth, endowed with the possibility of acquiring your own functioning of both fundamental sacred laws, have also the full possibility of coating in yourselves this most sacred particle of the Great All-Embracing of everything existing, and of perfecting it to the required degree of divine Reason. 'And this Great All-Embracing of all things embraced is called "Holy Prana." '

'If this most sacred Prana is crystallized in you, with the conscious or unconscious participation of your "I," you must without fail bring the perfecting of the individual Reason of this totality of most sacred atoms to the required gradation, otherwise this most holy coating, changing from one exterior coating to another, will suffer and languish eternally.

Each of the three-centered beings existing on the various planets of our Great Universe, and of course on the Earth also, must in reality be nothing other than a particle of that Most Great Greatness which is the All-Embracing of all that exists, and the foundation of this Most Great Greatness is there Above, the better to embrace the essence of everything existing.

definition: The Hrakhartzakha is an "exceedingly interesting apparatus" invented by the scientist Gornahoor Harharkh, serving as the "chief demonstrating part" of his invention for studying the cosmic substance Okidanokh. It is a large apparatus found in his workshop, designed to contain and observe cosmic processes. Its interior can be filled with a blinding light, which is a consequence of the striving of Okidanokh's parts to reblend into a whole in the "absolutely empty space" inside. Beings can be carried into it, fitted with special equipment, and connected to internal apparatuses. Once sealed, it isolates occupants from the entire Universe, and a pump can exhaust its contents to an absolute vacuum. It is used to demonstrate how various formations (like mineraloids, gases, metalloids, and metals) arise from minerals and transform one into another, allowing for observation of normally inscrutable "world laws." The objects within it are made of special materials invented by Gornahoor Harharkh.
role: An experimental apparatus used by a scientist to study and demonstrate cosmic laws and the processes of the omnipresent Okidanokh in an isolated, vacuum-like environment.

key_quotes.text: He pressed another button, and suddenly the whole interior of the 'hrakhartzakha' was filled with the same blinding light that I had experienced before, when I was standing outside this apparatus.

This flood of light had apparently been released when Gornahoor Harharkh, by pressing the button, had again opened the section of the wall of the 'hrakhartzakha' that permitted 'rays' to pass through. "He explained further that the light was a consequence of the 'striving to reblend into a whole' of the parts of Okidanokh, which was proceeding in the absolutely empty space inside the 'hrakhartzakha'; and it was manifested there owing to what is called 'reflection from outside,' that is to say, to the return of the rays to the place of their origin.
all the objects contained in this 'hrakhartzakha,' as well as everything we were to put on later, were made of special materials invented by Gornahoor Harharkh
the chief demonstrating part of which he called 'hrakhartzakha.'

the assistants of the great all-universal scientist Gornahoor Harharkh, again with the help of special appli-ances, carried us into the 'hrakhartzakha' itself. Then, having screwed all the free ends of the connectors projecting from us to the corresponding apparatuses in the 'hrakhartzakha,' they went out and hermetically sealed behind them the only way by which it would have been possible to have any communication with what is called 'all that represents a world.'

Yet thanks to my new invention and to the knowledge we have already attained, we now have the possibility not only of returning to the world, to become once again particles of all that exists, but also of being worthy to be nonparticipating eyewitnesses of certain world laws, which by ordinary uninitiated three-centered beings are considered inscrutable mysteries of Nature, but which in reality are natural and simple results automatically flowing one from another.'

definition: The term "ilnosoparnian" describes a manner or process in which the two fundamental sacred cosmic laws, the sacred Heptaparaparshinokh and the sacred Triamazikamno, in a specific cosmic concentration, are deflected independently and also manifest themselves on its surface independently, within certain limits. This "ilnosoparnian process" was actualized on the planet Earth, and on many other planets, with the sanction of His Endlessness and under the direction of the Great Archangel Sakaki. This process led to the arising of "everything corresponding to the 'ilnosoparno'," which allowed for the stable existence of detached planetary fragments, such as Loonderperzo (later called the Moon) and Anulios, preventing a universal catastrophe.
role: A cosmic manner or process where two sacred laws independently deflect and manifest, enabling the stable existence of detached planetary fragments.

key_quotes.text: mazikamno,' act in the 'ilnosoparnian' manner, that is to say, when these two sacred laws in a given cosmic concentration are deflected independently, and also manifest themselves on its surface independently—of course only within certain limits.

Afterward, when these Sacred Individuals had obtained the sanction of His Endlessness, the ilnosoparnian process was actualized on the planet Earth, still under the direction of the Great Archangel Sakaki. And so from that time on, on that planet, as on many others, there began to arise everything corresponding to the 'ilnosoparno,' thanks to which the detached fragments exist until now without constituting a threat of catastrophe on a universal scale.

definition: The "ilnosoparno" is what began to arise on planets, including Earth, following the "ilnosoparnian process." It is a state or set of conditions that enables detached fragments of planets, such as the larger fragment Loonderperzo (later called the Moon) and the smaller fragment Anulios, to exist without posing a threat of catastrophe on a universal scale.
role: The collective state or conditions that arose from the "ilnosoparnian process," enabling the stable existence of detached planetary fragments.

key_quotes.text: And so from that time on, on that planet, as on many others, there began to arise everything corresponding to the 'ilnosoparno,' thanks to which the detached fragments exist until now without constituting a threat of catastrophe on a universal scale.

definition: Inkiranondel is the name given to "incredible torments" that must be constantly endured by "Eternal Hasnamuss Individuals" on the planet Retribution. These torments are analogous to the suffering of "remorse of conscience," but are described as being "much more intense." The greatest anguish of this state for these highest being-bodies is that they must endure these terrible sufferings with the full consciousness that there is no hope whatever of them coming to an end.
role: A specific type of intense, eternal torment endured by Eternal Hasnamuss Individuals on the planet Retribution.

key_quotes.text: On that planet Retribution, the Eternal Hasnamuss Individuals must constantly endure those incredible torments called 'inkiranondel,' which are like the suffering of 'remorse of conscience' only much more intense.
The greatest anguish of this state of the highest being-bodies is that they must always endure these terrible sufferings, fully conscious that there is no hope whatever of their coming to an end.

definition: Inkliatzanikshanas refers to two distinct modes or tempos of "blood circulation" that begin to proceed in three-brained beings of Earth after they reach a certain age. These two "inkliatzanikshanas" are correlated with the beings' "two independent consciousnesses" (also called a "two-system zoostat"), which formed due to their abnormal existence. Each inkliatzanikshana evokes the functioning of one of these consciousnesses, and conversely, the intensive functioning of a consciousness evokes its corresponding mode of blood circulation. The difference between these modes depends on what is called the "tempodavlakshernian circulation" or "extent of filling of the blood vessels," where the center of gravity of blood pressure is situated in different parts of the general system of blood vessels depending on whether the being is in a "waking state" or a "passive state."
role: Two distinct modes of blood circulation in three-brained beings, correlated with their two independent consciousnesses, which arose due to abnormal conditions of existence.

key_quotes.text: after your favorites reach a certain age, there begin to proceed in them two 'inkliatzanikshanas' of different tempos or, as they themselves would say, two different modes of 'blood circulation.'

From this age on, each of these 'inkliatzanikshanas' or 'blood circulations' of different tempo evokes the functioning of one of their consciousnesses, and vice versa, the intensive functioning of either consciousness evokes the mode of 'blood circulation' corresponding to it.

The difference between these two independent modes of blood circulation in their common presence depends on what is called the 'tempodavlakshernian circulation' or, according to the expression of their contemporary 'medicine,' the 'extent of filling of the blood vessels'
